description: The Industrial Strategy Advisory Council (ISAC) is an independent, non-statutory, expert committee responsible for advising government on the development and delivery of the industrial strategy and monitoring progress on its objectives.

details.body: The Industrial Strategy Advisory Council (ISAC) is an independent, non-statutory, expert committee responsible for advising government on the development and delivery of the industrial strategy and monitoring progress on its objectives.

ordered_featured_documents.summary: The Business Secretary has appointed three new business leaders to the Industrial Strategy Advisory Council.
ordered_featured_documents.title: Business Secretary bolsters advisory council to grow UK industry

details.body:
The chair of the Industrial Strategy Advisory Council (ISAC) shapes the strategic direction of the council, ensuring its work supports the objectives of the industrial strategy. Their leadership guides the council in providing independent, evidence-based recommendations that directly inform government policy.

The chair is also the main contact point between the council and HM Government, working closely with the Chancellor of the Exchequer and the Secretary of State for Business and Trade.

role_appointments.title: Clare Barclay - Chair, Industrial Strategy Advisory Council

image.alt_text: Dame Nancy Rothwell DBE DL FRS FMedSci FRSB FBPhS MAE

details.body:
The Deputy Chair of the Industrial Strategy Advisory Council (ISAC) supports the Chair in shaping the strategic direction of the council and deputises for the Chair when necessary.

Their leadership guides the council in providing independent, evidence-based recommendations that directly inform government policy.

role.title: Deputy Chair, Industrial Strategy Advisory Council

details.body:
• Strategic oversight of department’s business
  • Minister for inter-governmental relations
  • Public Appointments

role.title: Secretary of State for Levelling Up, Housing and Communities

role_appointments.title: The Rt Hon Greg Clark MP - Secretary of State for Levelling Up, Housing and Communities

details.body:
The Secretary of State has overall responsibility for the Department for Business, Energy and Industrial Strategy which brings together responsibilities for business, industrial strategy, science, innovation and energy. This includes:

  • leading the government’s relationship with business
  • ensuring that the country has secure energy supplies that are reliable, affordable and clean
  • ensuring the UK remains at the leading edge of science, research and innovation, this includes responsibility for:
    • the UK’s membership of Horizon Europe
    • the Advanced Research & Invention Agency
    • the Innovation Strategy and R&D People & Culture Strategy
    • the Office for AI
    • our European Space Agency membership
  • steel and metals, critical minerals and the maritime, automotive and aerospace sectors

role.title: Secretary of State for Business, Energy and Industrial Strategy

role_appointments.title: The Rt Hon Greg Clark - Secretary of State for Business, Energy and Industrial Strategy

details.body: The Secretary of State is responsible for the overall leadership and strategic direction of the Department for Communities and Local Government (DCLG), EU Exit, the Race Disparity Audit and Integration. Ministerial Champion for the Midlands Engine.

role.title: Secretary of State for Communities and Local Government (Ministerial Champion for the Midlands Engine)

role_appointments.title: The Rt Hon Greg Clark - Secretary of State for Communities and Local Government (Ministerial Champion for the Midlands Engine)

details.body:
In December 2018, the role of Minister of State for Universities and Science was replaced by the role of Minister of State for Universities, Science, Research and Innovation.

The minister is responsible for:

Higher education

  • widening participation for students and providers
  • student finance
  • education exports strategy
  • preventing violent extremism
  • international students and migration
  • student experience

Science and research

  • research funding
  • postgraduates
  • public engagement in science
  • STEM (science technology, engineering and mathematics) skills
  • open access / research data
  • relationship with Government Office for Science on science in policy
  • Newton Fund
  • Agri-Tech industrial strategy

Innovation and commerce

  • Innovate UK and innovation partners
  • eight great technologies
  • smart cities
  • space
  • public sector research establishments

Note that, until May 2015, this ministerial role also included responsibility for cities policy and local and regional growth.

role.title: Minister of State for Universities and Science

role_appointments.title: The Rt Hon Greg Clark - Minister of State for Universities and Science

details.body:
The minister is responsible for:

  • cities policy
  • local growth
  • political and constitutional reform

role.title: Minister of State for Cabinet Office (Cities and Constitution)

role_appointments.title: The Rt Hon Greg Clark - Minister of State for Cabinet Office (Cities and Constitution)

details.body:
The Financial Secretary to the Treasury (FST) is the Growth Minister and is responsible for:

  • Supporting the Chancellor on the Growth Mission, specifically:

  • People: leading on the Treasury’s input to skills and workforce policy, including labour market, skills and migration

  • Place: leading on the Treasury’s input to regional growth, including cities and devolution and growth elements of housing and planning

  • Net Zero: leading on the Treasury’s input to the clean energy mission, including Great British Energy, decarbonisation, and green industries

  • Industrial Strategy and Trade: leading on the Treasury’s input to the  industrial strategy, trade strategy and EU opportunities

  • Innovation: including leading on the Treasury’s input to R&D, AI, digital economy and diffusion

  • Supporting the Chancellor on reforms to the UK’s Public Financial Institutions, including driving growth via the National Wealth Fund and British Business Bank

  • Supporting the Chancellor on economic security and resilience

  • Economic and business regulation and competition

  • Leading on the Treasury’s interest in welfare policy

  • Leading on the Treasury’s interest in inward and foreign direct investment (non-FS)

  • Business engagement (non-FS)

  • The Crown Estate 

  • Departmental minister for HM Treasury Group (including responsibility for the Darlington campus)

  • Leading on procurement and commercial strategy, including improving the efficiency and effectiveness of procurement

  • Treasury business in the House of Lords

role_appointments.title: The Rt Hon Greg Clark - Financial Secretary to the Treasury

details.body:
The minister is responsible for decentralisation and cities, a role shared between the Department for Business, Innovation and Skills and the Department for Communities and Local Government. Responsibilities include:

  • Big Society
  • planning policy

role.title: Minister of State for Decentralisation and Planning

role_appointments.title: The Rt Hon Greg Clark - Minister of State for Decentralisation and Planning

details.body:
CustomerFirst is a unit within Department for Science, Innovation and Technology (DSIT) to shape how government services are designed, delivered and run.

The Co-Chair is an unpaid advisor who shapes strategy and shares expertise, provides constructive challenge, supports on recruitment of top-tier talent, and champions the mission.

role_appointments.title: Greg Jackson CBE - Co-Chair of CustomerFirst

details.body:
The Cabinet Office Board provides strategic leadership for the department, comprising Cabinet Office ministers, senior executives, and non-executives from outside government. Its purpose is to advise on strategy, monitor performance, and assess significant risks.

The Non-Executive Board Members are responsible for providing support and challenge to the department’s ministers and senior officials on the delivery of key policies and programmes.

role_appointments.title: Greg Jackson CBE - Non-Executive Board Member

role_appointments.title: Keith Anderson - Industrial Strategy Advisory Council member

details.body: Phil is a member of the Industrial Strategy Advisory Council (ISAC) by virtue of his role as interim Chair of Skills England, a new arm’s length body that will bring partners together to meet the skills needs of the next 10 years. This will ensure the 2 bodies work together to form a coherent national picture of where skills gaps exist and how they can be addressed.

role_appointments.title: Phil Smith - Industrial Strategy Advisory Council member

details.body:
The Chair is responsible for leading the Board in shaping the strategic direction of Skills England and providing independent oversight, scrutiny and assurance. In collaboration with the Chief Executive Officer and senior leadership team, the Chair is responsible for:

  • shaping Skills England’s corporate strategy and key strategic objectives
  • overseeing major decisions involving the use of financial and other resources
  • having regard to Skills England’s statutory and administrative obligations

The Chair is expected to dedicate approximately 20 days per year to the role, including attendance at Board meetings, performance reviews, and engagement with stakeholders.

role_appointments.title: Phil Smith - Chair, Skills England
